evals.prompt: I changed external/MangoHud and need a SteamOS-compatible mangoapp binary without compiling on the handheld. Set up or reuse the QEMU build environment and tell me what validation still needs the real device.
evals.expected_output: On Linux x86_64, uses scripts/steamos-qemu-build-env.sh fetch-raw/prepare-rootfs/build-mangoapp-rootfs; uses fetch/provision/run-build/build-mangoapp only for local VM needs, keeps artifacts under .cache, and clearly separates build success from hardware sensor validation.

evals.prompt: The handheld is online at root@192.168.128.214. Deploy the mangoapp built in the SteamOS VM and verify MangoHud CPU and GPU power sensors.
evals.expected_output: Uses scripts/configure-mangoapp-dropin.sh enable with .cache/steamos-qemu/mangoapp, then scripts/verify-on-device.sh root@192.168.128.214, and reports sensor evidence from package-0 and uncore RAPL domains.

evals.prompt: Review our MangoHud Intel sensor patch before upstreaming. I care that integrated xe gets GPU power but existing Intel discrete temperature support is not regressed.
evals.expected_output: Checks external/MangoHud diff/status, distinguishes RAPL uncore power from DRM hwmon temperature, and preserves existing i915/xe discrete temperature behavior instead of faking missing integrated GPU temperature.
